history.content:
<thought>
I’m at the top-left area facing right (`>`). The goal is near the bottom-right. There’s lava (`L`) blocking a direct path along the row, so I should turn downward to work my way around the lava barrier toward the lower rows.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Now facing down (`v`). The cell directly below the current position is lava (`L`), which I must avoid. Instead, I should turn right to face right and look for an open floor path around the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m back facing right (`>`), and moving forward would hit lava at the next cell. To avoid the lava, I need to face down again and look for a safe route; turning left from right to down avoids the lava wall immediately in front.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing down (`v`). The cell in front is lava (`L`), so moving forward is unsafe. I need to change direction to go around; turning right will make me face left or right? From down, TURN_RIGHT makes me face left, which should avoid the lava in front.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing left (`<`). The cell in front is lava (`L`), so I can’t move forward. To avoid the lava, I should turn back to face down (`v`) and then navigate around it.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing down (`v`) and the cell in front is lava (`L`), so I must not move forward. I will turn right to change direction away from the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m back at the start row and facing left (`<`), with lava directly in front, so moving forward would fail. I need to face down (`v`) to get away from the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing up (`^`) and lava is directly in front, so I can’t move forward. I should turn right to face down away from the lava corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing right (`>`), and the cell in front is lava (`L`), so I should not move forward. To avoid the lava, I’ll turn right to face down (`v`).
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing down (`v`) but lava (`L`) is directly in front, so moving forward would be blocked. I should turn left to face right (`>`), where the next cell is floor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing right (`>`), and the cell ahead is lava (`L`), so I still can’t move forward. I need to face down (`v`) to try to go around.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing down (`v`) again, with lava (`L`) directly ahead, so I must turn away. Turning left will face right (`>`), but that also has lava ahead; however this step-wise rotation is needed to explore other directions.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m back facing right (`>`), and lava is immediately in front, so I cannot move forward. I’ll rotate to face down (`v`) to attempt a different route.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing down (`v`) and lava is in front again, so moving forward is blocked. I’ll turn left to face right (`>`), searching for a workable corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
Forward is still lava (`L`), so I cannot advance. I need to face a direction with free floor; I’ll turn to face up (`^`).
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m back facing down (`v`); the cell ahead is lava, so moving forward will fail. I should face right to find a path around the lava blocks.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT

<thought>
Forward is lava (`L`), so I can’t move. I should turn to face left (`<`) to look for a safe route.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
Facing up (`^`), the cell ahead is wall (`#`), so I can’t move forward. Turning right will face me toward the corridor where movement might be possible.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Forward is lava (`L`), so I should turn right to change direction away from the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT

<thought>
I’m facing down and the cell in front is lava (`L`), so moving forward would fail. I should turn left to avoid the lava and look for open floor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT

<thought>
I’m facing right again; directly in front is lava (`L`). I should turn down (left turn) to try another route away from the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT

<thought>
I’m facing up; the cell in front is lava (`L`) at the top row. I need to turn right to face away from the lava and look for floor.
</thought>
<action>

<thought>
Facing right again, lava is directly in front, so I must avoid moving forward. I’ll turn downward (right turn) to head toward the corridor below.
</thought>

<thought>

<thought>
I’m facing right, and the cell in front is floor (not lava or wall). I should move forward to progress along the open path.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
